name: pgvector
what_it_does: Open-source vector similarity search extension for PostgreSQL — lets you store, index, and query high-dimensional vector embeddings alongside relational data using standard SQL.
use_cases: Storing and querying LLM embeddings for RAG pipelines directly in Postgres
Semantic search over documents, products, or any embedded content
Recommendation systems using cosine similarity on user/item embeddings
Combining vector search with SQL filters (hybrid search) in a single query
Anomaly detection and duplicate detection on embedded data
not_for: Billion-scale ANN search (use Pinecone, Weaviate, or Milvus for massive scale)
Standalone vector database use (requires existing PostgreSQL installation)
Multi-modal search without a separate embedding model
best_when: Your agent already uses PostgreSQL and needs vector search — pgvector avoids a separate vector DB service, keeping your data in one place.
avoid_when: You need to search billions of vectors at millisecond latency — dedicated vector DBs with HNSW indexes outperform pgvector at very large scale.

auth.notes: Uses standard PostgreSQL authentication (md5, scram-sha-256, SSL cert). No separate auth layer — inherits Postgres roles and permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — open source PostgreSQL extension (MIT license)
pricing.notes: pgvector itself is free. Managed Postgres services with pgvector included: Supabase (free tier), Neon (free tier), AWS RDS (standard pricing), Railway, Render.

performance.rate_limits: None inherent — limited by your PostgreSQL server resources

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard PostgreSQL error messages with SQLSTATE codes. Extension errors are clear and consistent.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Standard SQL INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE semantics. ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (upsert) for idempotent vector inserts.
known_agent_gotchas: Must normalize vectors before cosine similarity if using inner product index
HNSW index is built once — inserting millions of vectors after index creation is slower
No automatic dimension validation — inserting wrong-dimension vector raises a SQL error
IVFFlat index requires approximate probes parameter tuning (lists/probes trade-off)
vector type casting required when inserting from Python numpy arrays (use .tolist())

security_score_components.security_notes: Inherits all PostgreSQL security features. TLS in-transit configurable (required with managed providers). Row-level security works with vector tables. MIT licensed with clean dependency tree.
